summary: Iter10 — Financial Ledger (double-entry on Mongo) HTTP API + LedgerAdminPage frontend tested end-to-end. 22/22 new ledger tests pass (chart of accounts, fee splits direct + 3p, validation 400s, webhook funding + idempotency, amount mismatch 400, release authz, payouts list scoping + admin disburse with MOCK_ ref, dispute lifecycle: open / second-open 400 / admin resolve refund / mutual buyer+seller agree). 42/42 prior iter5/6/8 regression tests still pass. Frontend /admin/ledger renders, fee calculator is reactive (200k → TZS 206,000 / 196,000 / 10,000 in direct, three_party reveals supplier_cost), tabs switch (payouts/disputes/accounts), empty states show (payouts-empty + disputes-empty), accounts table has 5 seeded rows.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~5040 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend extracting ledger endpoints into /app/backend/routers/ledger.py (and disputes / payouts into their own routers) before next iteration. The ledger.py module is already well-isolated; the HTTP layer should follow.

GatewayWebhookPayload has NO authentication — production AzamPay/Selcom webhooks must verify HMAC signatures. Currently any client knowing an order_id + matching gross_amount could fund an order. Mark this clearly as 'mock-only' until signature verification is added.

POST /api/orders/{id}/release does not verify that the order is not currently in a 'dispute' status — only that status == 'funded'. Once dispute flips order.status to 'dispute', release is correctly blocked. ✓

Dispute open allows seller to dispute (buyer or seller). However, /disputes/{id}/agree only checks role-vs-user matches; if buyer_id == seller_id (same user), one user could fully resolve a dispute alone. Edge case worth guarding (currently exploited by my mutual-agree test, but in prod buyer != seller).
Payouts have no DB unique index on payout_id; no double-disbursement guard beyond status check. Race-safe with status='queued'→'paid' update — recommend an atomic find_one_and_update pattern.

processed_webhooks lacks unique index on (provider, event_id) — idempotency guard is a read-then-insert, racy under concurrent webhook delivery. Add: db.processed_webhooks.create_index([('provider',1),('event_id',1)], unique=True).
ledger_entries lacks indexes on order_id + created_at — assert_balanced will scan the whole collection eventually. Recommend a compound index.

Frontend LedgerAdminPage is presented as 'admin' but the test (non-admin) user can fully view it. Disburse buttons render only for queued payouts which non-admin won't see, but accounts/disputes views should still be admin-gated server-side via the role check (currently they're scope-filtered, which is fine).
POST /ledger/quote is unauthenticated. Acceptable for a buyer-facing pricing widget but consider rate-limiting since it's CPU-cheap but enumerable.
test_credentials: +255712345678 / test1234 (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Tests temporarily promote this user to role='admin' in Mongo for admin-only endpoints, then revert.
seed_data_creation: Each ledger e2e test creates ephemeral TEST_ord_* orders directly in Mongo (because /api/orders/create is not yet refactored to use the new ledger — deferred per agent_to_agent_context_note). All TEST_ data is cleaned up via fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter10_ledger_http.py -v. Tests insert orders into the orders collection directly because /api/orders/create has NOT yet been wired to the ledger. When the next iter refactors order creation to call ledger_calculate_split + post_funds_received, replace the funded_order fixture's direct mongo.orders.insert_one with the real POST /api/orders endpoint and assert the same balanced-ledger invariants. Frontend admin page only assumes test user has 'admin' nav access (route is publicly accessible to logged-in users); if next iter adds a role gate, update the playwright login flow to seed an admin user first.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in iter10 scope. All 22 ledger HTTP tests + 42 regression tests + 5 frontend critical flows pass. Fee invariant gross == seller + agent + platform holds for both direct and three_party modes including in the live UI.
